title: audio_features.json — tempo, beats, onsets, envelopes, loudness, vocal activity
description: All dense series use the timeseries convention (common.schema.json) at a 10 ms hop and are sampled at arbitrary t via linear interpolation; beats/onsets are sorted event lists queried by binary search. This document owns the beat grid: every beat/bar index anywhere in the library refers to it.

bpm_global.description: Single representative tempo. Tempo drift shows up in the beat grid itself, not here.

confidence.description: Beat-tracker confidence; below ~0.5 treat the whole grid as advisory.

beats.description: The unified beat grid. Beat index i = times[i]. Bar index of beat i = floor((i - downbeat_offset) / beats_per_bar) once i >= downbeat_offset.

downbeat_offset.description: Index into times of the first downbeat; downbeats are then every beats_per_bar-th beat.

downbeat_method.description: assumed_4_4_phase_fit = estimated by choosing the beat phase maximizing onset strength under a 4/4 assumption; consumers should treat bar indices as approximate. 'model' reserved for a future real downbeat tracker.

integrated_lufs.description: EBU R128 integrated loudness of the mix; use to normalize levels across tracks.

stems.description: Fixed four Demucs stems — fixed keys so Swift decodes a concrete struct.

vocal_activity.description: Regions where the vocal stem is active, from hysteresis thresholding of its RMS envelope. lyrics.json measures its coverage against these.

mean_rms_db.description: Mean vocal-stem RMS within the region, dBFS.

spectral_centroid_hz.description: unit 'hz'; 0.0 where the frame is below the silence floor (undefined centroid).

strengths.description: Parallel to times; onset-envelope value normalized to this channel's max. Same length as times.
